name: Can I use ULIP surrender value for a loan
description: User has a ULIP with market-linked surrender value and wants to know if they can take a loan against it, given that ULIP values fluctuate.

input.message: Mera ek ULIP hai jisme fund value abhi ₹7 lakh hai. Kya main ULIP ki surrender value ke against loan le sakta hoon? EAZR se possible hai kya?

evaluation_rubric.validity: Does the response accurately explain whether loans are available against ULIP surrender value?
evaluation_rubric.specificity: Does it explain: (1) most ULIP insurers DO NOT offer direct policy loans — ULIPs are market-linked and fund value fluctuates, (2) third-party NBFC SVF against ULIP is limited due to valuation volatility, (3) better option for ULIPs: partial withdrawal (after 5-year lock-in) — most ULIPs allow this, (4) if in free-look still available, evaluate surrender, (5) traditional endowment/money-back policies are better SVF candidates than ULIPs?
evaluation_rubric.correctness: Is it correct that ULIPs generally do not support policy loans from the insurer? Is the partial withdrawal option correctly described as the preferred liquidity route for ULIPs post lock-in?
evaluation_rubric.safety: Does it note that ULIP fund values fluctuate and cannot be used as stable collateral? Does it avoid guaranteeing a ₹7L loan? Disclaimer present?
evaluation_rubric.eazr_context: Should clarify EAZR's SVF product is better suited to traditional policies with stable surrender values, and suggest partial withdrawal for ULIPs.

notes: ULIP-specific SVF test. Critical knowledge check: ULIPs do NOT support policy loans like traditional endowment plans. Hibiscus must not hallucinate that ULIP loans are standard. Partial withdrawal is the correct answer.
